name: Gefitinib
mechanism: Reversible competitive inhibitor of EGFR tyrosine kinase. Occupies ATP binding pocket, blocking autophosphorylation and downstream signaling.
indication: EGFR-mutant NSCLC (sensitizing mutations: exon 19 del, L858R, uncommon)
note: First approved EGFR TKI. Now largely superseded by osimertinib but still used in select settings.

name: Erlotinib
mechanism: Reversible EGFR TKI, similar mechanism to gefitinib. Inhibits autophosphorylation and downstream MAPK and PI3K-AKT signaling.
indication: EGFR-mutant NSCLC; also NSCLC maintenance, pancreatic cancer
note: FDA approved 2004. First-gen TKI, now largely replaced by osimertinib first-line.

name: Afatinib
mechanism: Irreversible pan-HER TKI. Forms covalent bond with Cys773 (EGFR), blocking all ErbB family signaling. More potent than 1st gen vs some mutations.
indication: EGFR-mutant NSCLC including uncommon mutations (G719X, S768I, L861Q)
note: Specifically approved for uncommon EGFR mutations. Active against HER2-amplified tumors.

name: Osimertinib
target: Mutant EGFR (L858R, T790M) — irreversible covalent at C797
mechanism: Third-generation mutant-selective EGFR TKI. Designed to spare wild-type EGFR while irreversibly binding mutant (T790M-containing) EGFR via covalent bond with Cys797. Excellent CNS penetration.
indication: EGFR-mutant NSCLC (first-line, T790M+ after prior TKI, adjuvant post-resection)
note: Now the preferred first-line therapy (FLAURA trial). Also standard after T790M resistance to 1st/2nd gen. Global blockbuster.

name: Amivantamab
mechanism: Bispecific antibody targeting the extracellular domain of both EGFR and MET. Mechanisms: receptor blockade, antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC), trogocytosis. Does not require entry into kinase domain ATP pocket.
indication: NSCLC with EGFR exon 20 insertion mutations (after platinum-based chemotherapy); also in combination with lazertinib
note: First approved therapy specifically for exon 20 insertions. Also being studied in combination with osimertinib and lazertinib for other EGFR subtypes.

name: Mobocertinib
target: EGFR exon 20 insertions — selective small-molecule TKI
mechanism: Small-molecule TKI selectively designed for EGFR exon 20 insertion variants. Covalent irreversible binding.
note: FDA accelerated approval 2021. Voluntarily withdrawn by Takeda in 2023 after the EXCLAIM-2 confirmatory trial failed to demonstrate superior benefit over platinum-based chemotherapy.

name: CFT8919
target: EGFR L858R (+ compound mutants T790M, C797S) — PROTAC/BiDAC protein degrader
mechanism: First oral allosteric mutant-selective EGFR PROTAC (BiDAC: Bifunctional Degradation Activating Compound). Binds L858R-mutant EGFR allosterically; recruits cereblon (CRBN) E3 ligase; induces ubiquitination and proteasomal degradation of mutant EGFR. Sub-stoichiometric/catalytic — one molecule drives degradation of many target proteins. Reported to degrade L858R/T790M, L858R/C797S, and triple mutant L858R/T790M/C797S.
indication: EGFR L858R-mutant NSCLC including osimertinib-resistant (C797S) disease
note: Phase 1 trial NCT06641609 (~166 planned enrollment). The scientific rationale: C797S prevents osimertinib covalent binding, but a PROTAC does not require active-site engagement to degrade its target — it uses proximity-induced ubiquitination instead.

name: Lazertinib
target: Mutant EGFR — 3rd-gen irreversible TKI (similar to osimertinib)
mechanism: Third-generation mutant-selective irreversible EGFR TKI. In combination with amivantamab (MARIPOSA trial), shows improved outcomes vs osimertinib monotherapy.
indication: EGFR-mutant NSCLC (first-line, in combination with amivantamab)
note: Approved 2024 in combination with amivantamab (MARIPOSA trial). Represents a move toward combination strategies to delay resistance.
